data.title: Logic Gates: Chapter 1
data.statement:
This is Chapter 1 of a Logic Gate Series.

Evaluate the output of a logic gate given its binary input values.
A logic gate takes one or two binary inputs (0 or 1) and produces a binary output according to a well‑defined rule.
The supported gates in this puzzle are:
BUFFER, NOT, AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR

Description of gates
BUFFER
A single‑input gate.
Its output is identical to its input.
If [[A]] is 0, the output is 0.
If [[A]] is 1, the output is 1.

NOT
A single‑input gate.
Its output is the logical negation of the input.
If [[A]] is 0, the output is 1.
If [[A]] is 1, the output is 0.

AND
A two‑input gate.
Its output is 1 only if both inputs are 1.
In every other case, the output is 0.

OR
A two‑input gate.
Its output is 1 if at least one of the inputs is 1.
It outputs 0 only when both inputs are 0.

NAND
A two‑input gate.
Its output is the opposite of the AND gate.
It outputs 0 only when both inputs are 1.
In all other cases, it outputs 1.

NOR
A two‑input gate.
Its output is the opposite of the OR gate.
It outputs 1 only when both inputs are 0.
In all other cases, it outputs 0.

XOR
A two‑input gate.
Its output is 1 when the inputs are different.
If both inputs are the same, the output is 0.

XNOR
A two‑input gate.
Its output is 1 when the inputs are equal.
If both inputs are 0, the output is 1.
If both inputs are 1, the output is also 1.
If the inputs differ, the output is 0.
data.constraints:
[[G]] ∈ {BUFFER, NOT, AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR}
[[A]], [[B]] ∈ {0, 1}
data.stubGenerator:
read G:word(20)
read inputs:string(20)
write answer
data.inputDescription:
<<Line 1:>> A string [[G]] — the gate name.
<<Line 2:>> Either

One integer [[A]] (for BUFFER, NOT), or
Two integers [[A]] and [[B]] (for all binary gates).
data.outputDescription: <<Line 1:>> A single integer (0 or 1) — the output of gate [[G]].

lastVersion.statementHTML:
Goal

This is Chapter 1 of a Logic Gate Series.

Evaluate the output of a logic gate given its binary input values.
A logic gate takes one or two binary inputs (0 or 1) and produces a binary output according to a well‑defined rule.
The supported gates in this puzzle are:
BUFFER, NOT, AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR

Description of gates
BUFFER
A single‑input gate.
Its output is identical to its input.
If A is 0, the output is 0.
If A is 1, the output is 1.

NOT
A single‑input gate.
Its output is the logical negation of the input.
If A is 0, the output is 1.
If A is 1, the output is 0.

AND
A two‑input gate.
Its output is 1 only if both inputs are 1.
In every other case, the output is 0.

OR
A two‑input gate.
Its output is 1 if at least one of the inputs is 1.
It outputs 0 only when both inputs are 0.

NAND
A two‑input gate.
Its output is the opposite of the AND gate.
It outputs 0 only when both inputs are 1.
In all other cases, it outputs 1.

NOR
A two‑input gate.
Its output is the opposite of the OR gate.
It outputs 1 only when both inputs are 0.
In all other cases, it outputs 0.

XOR
A two‑input gate.
Its output is 1 when the inputs are different.
If both inputs are the same, the output is 0.

XNOR
A two‑input gate.
Its output is 1 when the inputs are equal.
If both inputs are 0, the output is 1.
If both inputs are 1, the output is also 1.
If the inputs differ, the output is 0.
Input
Line 1: A string G — the gate name.
Line 2: Either

One integer A (for BUFFER, NOT), or
Two integers A and B (for all binary gates).
Output
Line 1: A single integer (0 or 1) — the output of gate G.
Constraints
G ∈ {BUFFER, NOT, AND, OR, NAND, NOR, XOR, XNOR}
A, B ∈ {0, 1}
Example
Input
BUFFER
0
Output
0
